nativeRange.habitat: Shallow sandy-bottomed protected areas, reef flats, and rocky substrates in tropical and subtropical coastal waters
nativeRange.biotope: Marine benthic; anchors via rhizoids (holdfasts) into sandbed or rockwork; found in the photic zone in sheltered coastal environments

imageCandidates.notes: Primary product image from Mosaic Macros listing. Only hobby trade source for this variety.

summary: False-Grape Caulerpa (Caulerpa cupressoides var. lycopodium) is a quick-growing display alga with distinctive grape-like upright blade formations. Like all Caulerpa species, it is allelopathic and carries invasive risk; sale and possession are banned in California.
careNotes:
False-Grape Caulerpa is a variety of Cactus Caulerpa (Caulerpa cupressoides var. lycopodium) distinguished by its grape-like cluster blade formations on upright fronds, rather than the denser cactus-spine branching of the parent species. It grows noticeably faster than typical Cactus Caulerpa — classified as a quick-growing alga — making regular harvesting an important part of ongoing care. Carbohydrate-binding proteins (lectins) with anti-inflammatory properties have been isolated from specimens collected in Brazil, though this has no direct bearing on aquarium husbandry. This variety is rarely offered in the hobby trade and is most reliably sourced from specialist macroalgae retailers. General water parameter targets follow standard reef conditions shared across the Caulerpaceae family.

ALLELOPATHY: Like all Caulerpa species, this variety is expected to release secondary metabolites (caulerpenyne, sesquiterpenes) as chemical defenses. These compounds can stress corals and competing algae when released into the water column. Physical separation from sensitive SPS corals (stony corals with small polyps) and regular use of activated carbon are generally advisable precautions.

GOING SEXUAL / REPRODUCTIVE CRASH: All Caulerpa species can undergo holocarpy — a mass reproductive event in which the organism converts its entire cellular contents into gametes and effectively dies. This can release large quantities of organic matter into the water column, causing an acute water-quality event capable of crashing a reef tank. Triggers include sharp nutrient drops, disrupted photoperiod, or rapid temperature swings. Mitigations: run refugium or tank lighting on a consistent schedule (some keepers use 24-hour lighting to suppress reproductive triggering); harvest regularly to keep the colony in active vegetative growth; maintain stable (not zero) nutrient levels; remove any specimen showing reproductive structures (white or translucent fronds, turbid release) immediately.

Captive specimens must never be released into natural waterways.

High lighting is generally considered preferable. Moderate water flow is generally advised. Standard reef water parameters (NSW-level salinity, consistent temperature) are recommended.

CROSS-REFERENCE: See cactus-caulerpa for the parent species C. cupressoides and caulerpa-prolifera for the Atlantic/Mediterranean blade-form sibling sharing allelopathy and crash caveats.
breedingNotes: Propagates in the aquarium via fragmentation — cuttings of the stolon or upright fronds have been reported to establish as new plants — and by vegetative tip growth along the stolon.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole hobby trade source for this variety. Provided: growth form (grape-like blade formations), growth rate (quick-growing), placement (display), invasive status, California ban, and shipping/color-recovery notes.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Caulerpa cupressoides
additional.notes: Taxonomy confirmed: Family Caulerpaceae, Order Bryopsidales, Class Ulvophyceae. Variety lycopodium confirmed as one of five recognized varieties. Native range (global tropical/subtropical: Americas, Africa, Indo-Pacific, Australia) confirmed. Sesquiterpene allelopathy documented. Lectins with anti-inflammatory properties isolated from var. lycopodium specimens (Pacheco, Brazil).

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
